data.title: WideOpenWest Investigates Suspicious Activity on Cloud Platform
data.description:
Wideopenwest ( (WOW) ) has shared an update.

WideOpenWest, Inc. recently identified suspicious activity on its back-office cloud platform and promptly initiated an incident response, disabling access to the affected application. The ongoing investigation has not revealed any material impact on the company’s operations or financial conditions, and no sensitive personal information appears to have been accessed by external parties.

More about Wideopenwest

WideOpenWest, Inc. operates in the telecommunications industry, providing broadband and cable services to residential and business customers. The company focuses on delivering high-speed internet, cable television, and digital phone services.

data.title: BlackRock Reduces Stake in AIB Group
data.description:
The latest update is out from AIB Group ( (AIBRF) ).

AIB Group plc, a non-UK issuer, has reported a change in major holdings due to BlackRock, Inc.’s acquisition or disposal of voting rights and financial instruments. As of March 26, 2025, BlackRock’s total voting rights in AIB Group have decreased to 8.47% from a previous 10.18%, indicating a reduction in their stake in the company. This change in holdings may impact AIB Group’s shareholder dynamics and could influence future corporate decisions.

More about AIB Group

data.title: Cannabis Sativa Delays Form 10-K Filing
data.description:
Cannabis Sativa ( (CBDS) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Cannabis Sativa, Inc. has announced a delay in filing its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the financial period ending December 31, 2024. The primary reason for the delay is the recent engagement of a new independent registered public accounting firm on December 18, 2024, which requires additional time to review and approve the company’s financial statements. The company anticipates filing the delayed report by April 14, 2025. There are no expected significant changes in financial results from the previous fiscal year. The company is committed to ensuring compliance with applicable accounting standards and regulations, as indicated by the signature of CEO David Tobias on the notification.

More about Cannabis Sativa

data.title: Raffles Education Completes Sixth Tranche Bond Issuance
data.description:
An update from Raffles Education ( (SG:NR7) ) is now available.

Raffles Education Limited has completed the issuance of S$1,000,000 in non-convertible bonds to Sixth Tranche Subscribers, bringing the total issued bonds to S$18,250,000. This bond issuance is part of the company’s strategy to raise funds, potentially impacting its financial stability and market positioning, while shareholders are advised to exercise caution in dealing with company shares.

More about Raffles Education

Raffles Education Limited, incorporated in Singapore, operates in the education sector, providing educational services and programs through its subsidiaries. The company focuses on delivering quality education and training across various disciplines, catering to a diverse student population.

data.title: Heliostar Metals Secures $19.5 Million in Equity Financing
data.description:
The latest update is out from Heliostar Metals ( (TSE:HSTR) ).

Heliostar Metals Ltd. successfully closed a bought deal equity financing, raising $19.5 million, which includes a significant investment from Eric Sprott. The funds will be used for the development of their projects in Mexico, particularly drilling at Ana Paula, and for general corporate purposes. This financing move is expected to bolster the company’s operational capabilities and enhance its market position in the gold production industry.

More about Heliostar Metals

Heliostar Metals Ltd. is focused on becoming a mid-tier gold producer, with efforts concentrated on increasing production and developing new resources at the La Colorada and San Agustin mines in Mexico, as well as the 100% owned Ana Paula Project in Guerrero, Mexico.

data.title: LBG Media Plc Successfully Passes All Resolutions at 2025 AGM
data.description:
LBG Media Plc ( (GB:LBG) ) just unveiled an update.

LBG Media Plc announced that all resolutions proposed at its 2025 Annual General Meeting were approved by the necessary majority. Key resolutions included the election of directors and the re-appointment of auditors, as well as authorizations related to securities and share purchases. This successful AGM outcome supports LBG Media’s operational continuity and strategic initiatives, reinforcing its position in the digital entertainment industry and potentially benefiting stakeholders by maintaining strong governance and financial oversight.

More about LBG Media Plc

LBG Media Plc is a global digital entertainment company focused on engaging young adults through social media platforms like Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, X, YouTube, and TikTok, as well as its owned websites. The company produces and distributes digital content such as videos, editorials, images, and audio through brands like LADbible and SPORTbible, catering to popular interests like news, sports, and gaming. LBG Media generates revenue through creating bespoke content for advertisers and sharing advertising revenue with social media platforms.

data.title: Endava Reports Revenue Growth but Faces Profitability Challenges
data.description:
An update from Endava ( (DAVA) ) is now available.

Endava PLC reported its condensed consolidated interim results for the six months ending December 31, 2024, revealing a revenue increase to £390.6 million from £372 million in the previous year. Despite the revenue growth, the company experienced a decline in operating profit to £6.4 million from £20.7 million, attributed to increased selling, general, and administrative expenses. The total comprehensive income for the period showed a loss of £4.7 million, compared to a profit of £22.6 million in the prior year, indicating challenges in maintaining profitability amidst rising costs.

More about Endava

Endava PLC is a technology company based in London, specializing in IT services and consulting. The company focuses on delivering digital transformation solutions to clients across various industries.

data.title: Rosie’s Burgers Expands with New Franchise Agreement in British Columbia
data.description:
An announcement from Happy Belly Food Group ( (TSE:HBFG) ) is now available.

Happy Belly Food Group’s Rosie’s Burgers has announced a significant expansion through a multi-unit franchise agreement for five new stores in British Columbia. This marks the brand’s entry into the province and is part of a larger strategy to expand nationally, with 85 units already committed across Canada. The agreement underscores the brand’s strong market position and the effectiveness of its franchise support system, highlighting the company’s strategic growth in the Canadian QSR market.

More about Happy Belly Food Group

Happy Belly Food Group Inc. is a leading consolidator of emerging food brands, focusing on the quick-service restaurant (QSR) industry. The company is known for its diverse portfolio of brands, including Rosie’s Burgers, which offers handcrafted smash burgers, fries, and milkshakes.

data.title: China Longyuan Power Group Secures New Financing Lease Agreement
data.description:
China Longyuan Power Group ( (HK:0916) ) has provided an update.

China Longyuan Power Group has entered into a new Financing Lease Related Services Framework Agreement with Guoneng Financial, a subsidiary of its controlling shareholder CHN Energy. This agreement, effective from March 29, 2025, to December 31, 2027, involves providing financing lease services, including direct lease and sales and leaseback. The transactions are classified as continuing connected transactions under Hong Kong Listing Rules, requiring annual reporting and announcements but exempt from independent shareholders’ approval.
